text: The surgeon creates a preperitoneal plane starting on the left side of the midline and extends it across the midline. On the right side, the posterior rectus sheath is incised to enter the retrorectus plane, which is then extended laterally. The lateral posterior rectus sheath is incised, re-entering the preperitoneal plane. Dissection reveals the abdominal wall defect and the intact external oblique muscle as the peritoneum is carefully separated and released from the area of weakness.

The surgeon dissects over the transplanted kidney to create lateral overlap for mesh placement. After achieving the overlap, the fascial defect is closed with a running number one Stratafix barbed suture. An eighteen by five centimeters soft barred mesh is inserted to reinforce the primary repair, secured with interrupted two O Vicryl sutures.

A 5mm trocar is inserted into the right upper quadrant to introduce a 19 French Blake drain into the extraperitoneal space adjacent to the mesh. The peritoneum is closed with a running 2-0 V-lock barbed suture, and the surgical drain is opened to the air to evacuate CO2 from the extraperitoneal space.
